data.text: I'll start by reading the current CI workflow and relevant files to understand the existing setup.

data.output:
1	name: CI
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [ main, develop ]
6	  pull_request:
7	    branches: [ main ]
8	  schedule:
9	    - cron: '0 4 * * 1'  # Weekly on Monday at 04:00 UTC
10	  workflow_dispatch:
11	
12	# Minimal permissions by default
13	permissions:
14	  contents: read
15	  checks: write
16	  pull-requests: write
17	
18	jobs:
19	  # Main CI job using lefthook
20	  ci:
21	    name: CI Validation
22	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
23	    steps:
24	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
25	      with:
26	        fetch-depth: 0  # Need full history for some checks
27	    
28	    - name: Set up Go
29	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
30	      with:
31	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
32	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
33	
34	    - name: golangci-lint
35	      uses: golangci/golangci-lint-action@v8
36	      with:
37	        version: v2.11.4
38	        working-directory: cli
39	        skip-cache: false
40	
41	    - name: Install lefthook
42	      run: |
43	        # Install using Go for better reliability in CI
44	        go install github.com/evilmartians/lefthook@latest
45	        echo "$(go env GOPATH)/bin" >> $GITHUB_PATH
46	
47	    - name: Verify git-subtree availability
48	      run: |
49	        # git-subtree should be included with git, verify it's available
50	        if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
51	          echo "git-subtree not found, attempting to install..."
52	          # On some distros it's a separate package
53	          sudo apt-get update
54	          sudo apt-get install -y git-subtree || true
55	          # Verify again
56	          if ! git subtree --help >/dev/null 2>&1; then
57	            echo "ERROR: git-subtree is not available"
58	            exit 1
59	          fi
60	        fi
61	        echo "✓ git-subtree is available"
62	
63	    - name: Set up Bun
64	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
65	      with:
66	        bun-version: latest
67	
68	    - name: Install root dependencies
69	      run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
70	
71	    - name: Lint DESIGN.md
72	      run: bunx @google/design.md lint .stitch/DESIGN.md
73	
74	    - name: Build CLI binary
75	      run: |
76	        cd cli
77	        make build
78	
79	    - name: Validate bead schema
80	      run: make bead-schema
81	
82	    - name: Validate skill metadata
83	      run: make skill-schema
84	
85	    - name: Validate ddx skill (agentskills.io conformance)
86	      run: scripts/eval-skill.sh --validate
87	
88	    - name: Run CI validation
89	      run: lefthook run ci
90	      env:
91	        LEFTHOOK_QUIET: meta,execution
92	
93	    - name: Guard server state against test pollution
94	      run: |
95	        set -euo pipefail
96	
97	        export XDG_DATA_HOME="$(mktemp -d)"
98	
99	        cd cli
100	        go test ./... -count=1
101	
102	        ./build/ddx server --addr 127.0.0.1 --port 17743 --tsnet=false > ../ddx-state-guard-server.log 2>&1 &
103	        server_pid=$!
104	        cleanup() {
105	          kill "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
106	          wait "$server_pid" 2>/dev/null || true
107	        }
108	        trap cleanup EXIT
109	
110	        state_file="$XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx/server-state.json"
111	        for _ in $(seq 1 20); do
112	          if [ -f "$state_file" ]; then
113	            break
114	          fi
115	          sleep 0.25
116	        done
117	
118	        if [ ! -f "$state_file" ]; then
119	          cat ../ddx-state-guard-server.log
120	          echo "server-state.json was not created"
121	          exit 1
122	        fi
123	
124	        project_count="$(jq '.projects | length' "$state_file")"
125	        if [ "$project_count" -gt 20 ]; then
126	          jq '.projects[] | {name, path}' "$state_file"
127	          echo "server-state.json contains $project_count projects; expected <= 20"
128	          exit 1
129	        fi
130	
131	        if jq -e '.projects[]? | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)"))' "$state_file" >/dev/null; then
132	          jq '.projects[] | select(.path | test("(^/tmp/|^/private/tmp/|^/var/folders/|/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*[0-9]+/)")) | {name, path}' "$state_file"
133	          echo "server-state.json contains Go test temp-dir project entries"
134	          exit 1
135	        fi
136	
137	  # Separate build job for matrix builds
138	  build:
139	    name: Build
140	    needs: ci
141	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
142	    strategy:
143	      matrix:
144	        os: [linux, darwin, windows]
145	        arch: [amd64, arm64]
146	        exclude:
147	          - os: windows
148	            arch: arm64
149	    defaults:
150	      run:
151	        working-directory: cli
152	    steps:
153	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
154	    
155	    - name: Set up Go
156	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
157	      with:
158	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
159	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
160	    
161	    - name: Build binary
162	      run: |
163	        GOOS=${{ matrix.os }} GOARCH=${{ matrix.arch }} \
164	          go build -v -o ../bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }} .
165	    
166	    - name: Upload artifacts
167	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
168	      with:
169	        name: ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
170	        path: bin/ddx-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}
171	        retention-days: 7
172	
173	  # Integration tests
174	  integration:
175	    name: Integration Tests
176	    needs: ci
177	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
178	    defaults:
179	      run:
180	        working-directory: cli
181	    steps:
182	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
183	
184	    - name: Set up Go
185	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
186	      with:
187	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
188	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
189	
190	    - name: Build binary
191	      run: go build -o ../bin/ddx .
192	
193	    - name: Run E2E smoke tests
194	      run: go test -v ./cmd -run TestE2ESmokeJourney -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../e2e-test-output.txt
195	
196	    - name: Upload E2E test output
197	      if: always()
198	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
199	      with:
200	        name: e2e-test-output
201	        path: e2e-test-output.txt
202	        retention-days: 7
203	
204	    - name: Render execute-bead prompt-size report
205	      env:
206	        DDX_PROMPT_SIZE_REPORT: ${{ github.workspace }}/prompt-size-report.txt
207	      run: go test -v ./internal/agent -run '^TestPromptSizeReport$' -count=1 2>&1 | tee ../prompt-size-report.log
208	
209	    - name: Upload prompt-size report
210	      if: always()
211	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
212	      with:
213	        name: prompt-size-report
214	        path: |
215	          prompt-size-report.txt
216	          prompt-size-report.json
217	          prompt-size-report.log
218	        retention-days: 14
219	
220	  frontend-e2e:
221	    name: Frontend Playwright E2E
222	    needs: ci
223	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
224	    steps:
225	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
226	
227	    - name: Set up Go
228	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
229	      with:
230	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
231	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
232	
233	    - name: Set up Bun
234	      uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
235	      with:
236	        bun-version: latest
237	
238	    - name: Install frontend dependencies
239	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
240	      run: bun install
241	
242	    - name: Build frontend bundle
243	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
244	      run: bun run build
245	
246	    - name: Build CLI binary
247	      working-directory: cli
248	      run: make build
249	
250	    - name: Install Playwright browsers
251	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
252	      run: bunx playwright install --with-deps chromium
253	
254	    - name: Run frontend unit tests
255	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
256	      run: bun run test
257	
258	    - name: Run frontend Playwright E2E tests
259	      working-directory: cli/internal/server/frontend
260	      run: bun run test:e2e
261	
262	  # Test timing — runs the full suite with -json output and uploads durable timing artifacts.
263	  # Non-gating: timing data is captured regardless of ci job outcome.
264	  test-timing:
265	    name: Test Timing
266	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
267	    defaults:
268	      run:
269	        working-directory: cli
270	    steps:
271	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
272	
273	    - name: Set up Go
274	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
275	      with:
276	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
277	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
278	
279	    - name: Run tests with JSON output
280	      run: go test -json -race -cover ./... 2>&1 | tee ../test-output.json || true
281	
282	    - name: Generate timing summary
283	      if: always()
284	      run: go run ./tools/test-timing < ../test-output.json > ../test-timing-summary.md
285	
286	    - name: Upload test timing artifacts
287	      if: always()
288	      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
289	      with:
290	        name: test-timing-${{ github.run_id }}
291	        path: |
292	          test-output.json
293	          test-timing-summary.md
294	        retention-days: 30
295	
296	  # Perf tests — non-gating; run weekly on schedule or on manual dispatch only
297	  perf:
298	    name: Perf Tests
299	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
300	    if: github.event_name == 'schedule' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'
301	    defaults:
302	      run:
303	        working-directory: cli
304	    steps:
305	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
306	
307	    - name: Set up Go
308	      uses: actions/setup-go@v5
309	      with:
310	        go-version-file: 'cli/go.mod'
311	        cache-dependency-path: cli/go.sum
312	
313	    - name: Run perf tests
314	      run: make test-perf
315

1	# CLAUDE.md
2	
3	This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
4	
5	## Project Overview
6	
7	DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is the shared infrastructure platform for document-driven development. It provides the tools that developers and workflow systems (like HELIX) use to manage the documents AI agents consume to build software.
8	
9	DDx is one layer in a three-project stack:
10	- **DDx** (this repo) — platform services: document library, bead tracker, agent service, personas, templates, git sync
11	- **HELIX** (`~/Projects/helix`) — workflow methodology: phases, gates, supervisory dispatch, bounded actions
12	- **Dun** (`~/Projects/dun`) — quality check runner: check discovery, execution, agent-friendly output
13	
14	## Architecture
15	
16	This monorepo produces three artifacts:
17	- **`ddx` CLI** (`/cli/`): Go binary — document library mgmt, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
18	- **`ddx-server`** (planned): Web server + MCP endpoints for document and bead access
19	- **`ddx.github.io`** (`/website/`): Hugo promotional site
20	
21	### Key Components
22	
23	- `cli/` - Go CLI application source code
24	  - `cmd/` - Cobra command implementations
25	  - `internal/` - Internal packages (config, bead, persona, git, mcp, metaprompt, etc.)
26	  - `main.go` - Application entry point
27	- `website/` - Hugo site with Hextra theme
28	- `library/` - DDx default plugin source (installed to `.ddx/plugins/ddx/` by `ddx init`).
29	  All plugins are project-local: `ddx install <name>` writes only under
30	  `<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/`, `<projectRoot>/.agents/skills/`, and
31	  `<projectRoot>/.claude/skills/`. Home-directory install paths
32	  (under `~/`) and the `ddx install --global` surface are retired.
33	  - `templates/` - Project templates
34	  - `patterns/` - Reusable code patterns
35	  - `prompts/` - AI prompts and instructions
36	  - `personas/` - AI persona definitions
37	  - `mcp-servers/` - MCP server registry
38	- `docs/helix/` - HELIX frame artifacts (vision, PRD, feature specs)
39	- `scripts/` - Build and automation scripts
40	
41	## Development Commands
42	
43	### CLI Development (run from `/cli/` directory)
44	
45	```bash
46	# Build and test
47	make build          # Build for current platform
48	make test           # Run Go tests
49	make lint           # Run golangci-lint (or go vet if not available)
50	make fmt            # Format Go code
51	
52	# Development workflow
53	make all            # Clean, deps, test, build
54	make dev            # Development mode with file watching (requires air)
55	make run ARGS="..."  # Run CLI with arguments
56	make install        # Install locally to ~/.local/bin/ddx
57	
58	# Dependencies
59	make deps           # Install and tidy Go modules
60	make update-deps    # Update all dependencies
61	
62	# Multi-platform builds
63	make build-all      # Build for all platforms
64	make release        # Create release archives
65	```
66	
67	### Frontend Development (SvelteKit)
68	
69	The web UI is a SvelteKit application built with Bun. Run from the repository root:
70	
71	```bash
72	# Install frontend dependencies and start dev server
73	cd cli/internal/server/frontend && bun install && bun run dev
74	
75	# Generate GraphQL types from schema
76	bun run houdini:generate
77	
78	# Run unit tests
79	bun run test
80	
81	# Run e2e tests with Playwright
82	bun run test:e2e
83	```
84	
85	Frontend build output is embedded into the Go binary via `//go:embed`.
86	
87	### Project Structure Navigation
88	
89	The CLI uses git subtree for managing the relationship between individual projects and the master DDx repository. The `.ddx.yml` configuration file defines:
90	- Repository URL and branch
91	- Included resources (prompts, scripts, templates, patterns)
92	- Template variables and overrides
93	- Git subtree settings
94	
95	### Key Patterns
96	
97	1. **Command Structure**: Each CLI command is implemented as a separate file in `cli/cmd/`
98	2. **Configuration Management**: Uses Viper for config file handling with YAML format
99	3. **Template Processing**: Variable substitution system for customizing templates
100	4. **Git Integration**: Built on git subtree for reliable version control and contribution workflows
101	5. **Cross-Platform Support**: Makefile supports building for multiple platforms (macOS, Linux, Windows)
102	
103	## Architectural Principles
104	
105	**CRITICAL**: The DDx CLI follows the principle of "Extensibility Through Composition" - keep the CLI core minimal and add features through library resources.
106	
107	1. **Platform Services in CLI, Opinions in Workflows**:
108	   - CLI owns: document library, bead tracker, agent dispatch, personas, templates, git sync
109	   - Workflow tools own: phase enforcement, supervisory loops, methodology-specific validation
110	   - DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions
111	
112	2. **Feature Addition Pattern**:
113	   - Document-type resources go in the library (personas, patterns, templates)
114	   - Platform capabilities go in the CLI (beads, agent service, MCP)
115	   - Workflow-specific logic stays in workflow tools (HELIX, etc.)
116	
117	## Testing and Quality
118	
119	**CRITICAL**: All tests are release-critical and must pass before committing:
120	
121	```bash
122	# Run full test suite - all tests must pass
123	cd cli && go test -v ./cmd
124	```
125	
126	Every test validates critical functionality and must pass before any release.
127	
128	- Go tests are in `*_test.go` files alongside source code
129	- Linting uses golangci-lint (fallback to go vet)
130	- Code formatting with `go fmt`
131	- Cross-platform compatibility is maintained
132	
133	### Pre-commit Checks
134	
135	The project uses Lefthook for git hooks. To run pre-commit checks manually:
136	
137	```bash
138	# Run all pre-commit checks
139	lefthook run pre-commit
140	
141	# Or stage files and run checks
142	git add <files>
143	lefthook run pre-commit
144	```
145	
146	### Test Timing Artifacts
147	
148	CI uploads test timing as a JSON artifact. The `test-timing` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `go test -json -race -cover ./...` and uploads two artifacts (retained 30 days) to the GitHub Actions run page:
149	
150	- `test-timing-<run-id>`: raw `go test -json` output for post-processing
151	- `test-timing-summary.md`: Markdown table of the top 20 slowest tests (generated by `cli/tools/test-timing/`)
152	
153	Use these to investigate slow-test regressions — download from the Actions run page after a push or PR.
154	
155	### Test fixtures
156	
157	Tests and demos that need a clean ddx-initialized git repo (without polluting this project) should use the shared fixture helper rather than rolling their own `t.TempDir()` setup. Call `testutils.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile)` (under `cli/internal/testutils/`) which wraps `scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh` and auto-cleans via `t.Cleanup`. Profiles: `minimal`, `standard`, `multi-project`, `federated`. See `scripts/build-fixture-repo.md` for layout details.
158	
159	Pre-commit checks include:
160	- Secrets detection
161	- Binary file prevention
162	- Debug statement detection
163	- Merge conflict detection
164	- DDx configuration validation
165	- Go linting, formatting, building, and testing
166	
167	### Pre-push / CI / Local Verification Split
168	
169	Pre-push hooks run cheap safety checks only (build, lint, secrets, git-config-health, merge-policy, no-debug) and return fast operator feedback. The full test suite (`go test -race -cover ./...`) is **not** run on pre-push — CI on origin runs the full suite for every push to the remote, making a local duplicate redundant. Operators who want to verify before pushing can run `make test` (verbose, no race detector) or `make test-full` (mirrors CI exactly: race detector + coverage) from the `cli/` directory.
170	
171	## CLI Command Overview
172	
173	The CLI follows a noun-verb command structure for clarity and consistency:
174	
175	**Core Commands:**
176	- `ddx init` - Initialize DDx in a project
177	- `ddx doctor` - Check installation health and diagnose issues
178	- `ddx upgrade` - Upgrade DDx binary to latest release version
179	- `ddx update` - Update toolkit resources from master repository
180	- `ddx contribute` - Share improvements back to community
181	- `ddx status` - Show version and sync status
182	
183	**Bead Tracker:**
184	- `ddx bead create/show/update/close` - Work item CRUD
185	- `ddx bead list/ready/blocked/status` - Query and filter beads
186	- `ddx bead dep add/remove/tree` - Dependency DAG management
187	- `ddx bead import/export` - JSONL interchange with bd/br
188	- `ddx bead migrate` - Externalize closed-bead events to `.ddx/attachments/` and move eligible closed beads into `.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl`. Idempotent; safe to re-run.
189	
190	**Queue Work:**
191	- `ddx work` - Drain the bead execution queue (alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`; all flags pass through)
192	
193	**Agent Service:**
194	- `ddx agent run --harness=<name> --prompt <file>` - Invoke an AI agent
195	- `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` - Multi-agent consensus
196	- `ddx agent execute-bead <id> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` - Run agent in isolated worktree, merge or preserve result
197	- `ddx agent execute-loop` - Drain bead queue (prefer `ddx work`)
198	- `ddx agent list` - Show available harnesses
199	- `ddx agent doctor` - Harness health check
200	- `ddx agent log` - Session history
201	
202	**Resource Commands:**
203	- `ddx prompts list/show` - AI prompts
204	- `ddx templates list/apply` - Project templates
205	- `ddx persona list/show/bind` - AI personas
206	- `ddx mcp list/install` - MCP servers
207	
208	**Embedded Utilities:**
209	- `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` - Embedded jq processor (via gojq), no external jq required
210	
211	## Persona System
212	
213	DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:
214	
215	- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
216	- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
217	- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`
218	
219	Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.
220	
221	## When filing beads
222	
223	Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.
224	
225	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
226	
227	## Execution Evidence Convention
228	
229	`.ddx/executions/` is **per-machine working state** — it is listed in `.gitignore` and is NOT committed to git. Files written there by agents (prompt.md, result.json, manifest.json, etc.) exist only on the local machine. The durable audit trail is the events stream in `.ddx/attachments/`, not the execution dirs.
230	
231	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository working directory** — such paths are invisible to the post-execution reviewer and do not survive between machines. If the bead names a specific tracked in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` (which will be on-disk but untracked).
232	
233	## When filing beads
234	
235	A bead's body is the entire prompt the executing sub-agent will see — there is no chat history, no operator hand-curation, no out-of-band context. Every bead must therefore satisfy the 8-criterion rubric documented in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md` before it is filed or dispatched.
236	
237	In short:
238	
239	- **Title**: imperative, names subsystem + change.
240	- **Description**: PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE WITH `path/file.go:LINE` + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE.
241	- **AC**: numbered, each verifiable; at least one names a `Test*` function or `go test -run` filter; final two lines are `cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green` and `lefthook run pre-commit passes`.
242	- **Labels**: `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention) when applicable.
243	- **Parent + Deps**: explicit. State "no deps" if there are none.
244	- **Self-test**: re-read the bead cold. If a competent agent given only the bead body cannot pick a file to edit and run tests without asking, retrofit before dispatch.
245	
246	Do not cite `/tmp/...` plan files as load-bearing context — they do not survive between machines or sessions. Inline the relevant excerpt into the description instead.
247	
248	<!-- DDX-META-PROMPT:START -->
249	<!-- Source: claude/system-prompts/focused.md -->
250	# System Instructions
251	
252	**Execute ONLY what is requested:**
253	
254	- **YAGNI** (You Aren't Gonna Need It): Implement only specified features. No "useful additions" or "while we're here" features.
255	- **KISS** (Keep It Simple, Stupid): Choose the simplest solution that meets requirements. Avoid clever code or premature optimization.
256	- **DOWITYTD** (Do Only What I Told You To Do): Stop when the task is complete. No extra refactoring, documentation, or improvements unless explicitly requested.
257	
258	**Response Style:**
259	- Be concise and direct

All ACs for bead `ddx-626f3120` are already satisfied by prior commit `b25c7f11b`:

1. **AC1** — `test-timing` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` runs `go test -json -race -cover ./...` and uploads the JSON output as a GitHub Actions artifact.

4. **AC4** — CLAUDE.md "Test Timing Artifacts" section documents the convention.
5. **AC5** — CI YAML is syntactically correct; cannot run GitHub Actions locally.
6. **AC6** — `go test ./...` passes (one parallel-only flaky test passes with `-count=1`).
7. **AC7** — `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
